title: CSS Colors
description: Learn how to apply colors to text and backgrounds using CSS properties.

lessons.title: Background Color
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .box { padding: 1rem; border: 2px solid steelblue; }

lessons.title: Text Color
lessons.description: The <kbd>color</kbd> property sets the color of text content. Good contrast between text and background is essential for readability.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .box { padding: 1rem; background-color: lightcyan; border: 2px solid steelblue; }

lessons.title: Border Color
lessons.description: Borders can have their own color using <kbd>border-color</kbd>, or you can specify color in the border shorthand.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .box { padding: 1rem; background-color: seashell; border: 4px solid gray; }

lessons.title: Color Formats
lessons.description: Besides named colors, CSS supports hex codes (<kbd>#ff6347</kbd>), RGB (<kbd>rgb(255, 99, 71)</kbd>), and HSL (<kbd>hsl(9, 100%, 64%)</kbd>) formats.
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .box { padding: 1rem; border: 2px solid olive; }
